checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Revenues=9024900000 (FY2012) grew only ~8.7% YoY while combined Goodwill+Intangibles grew from 2954.3M (FY2011) to 5359.5M (FY2012), +81%

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=92300000 (2013-12-31) vs Revenues=10771900000 (FY2013) -> ratio ~0.85%, declining in line with a growing, more efficient revenue base; no COGS/gross margin data provided for further verification

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities=13904978000 (2009-12-31) growing to Liabilities=21817200000 (2013-12-31), broadly tracking asset/debt growth; no specific allowance for doubtful accounts, warranty, or restructuring reserve line items were provided in the dataset

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2009-03-03 (same day as the 10-K filed 2009-03-03)
NT 10-K filed 2013-03-04 (same day as the 10-K filed 2013-03-04)
NT 10-K filed 2014-03-03; 10-K filed 2014-03-19; 10-K/A filed 2014-03-20 -- an amendment filed only one day after the original annual report

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Core series (Revenues, NetIncomeLoss/ProfitLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, AccountsReceivableNet, Assets, Liabilities, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill) span FY2008-FY2013 with quarterly granularity, but allowance for doubtful accounts, COGS/gross margin detail, and specific reserve balances are absent from the dataset, limiting CL4 and CL6 analysis

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase price allocation / capitalization adjustments related to the Dollar Thrifty acquisition (closed Nov 2012) appear to have been substantially revised between the originally reported interim/annual figures and the 10-K/A refiling, with Goodwill and Intangibles roughly tripling and doubling respectively versus prior quarterly disclosures; the immediate one-day amendment of the FY2013 10-K (filed 2014-03-19, amended 2014-03-20) suggests these balances required correction, consistent with capitalized-item misstatement risk.

Receivables appear to have grown faster than revenue in 2011-2012 (potential overstatement/aggressive revenue-related receivable recognition or delayed write-offs), followed by an unusual sharp reversal in 2013 where receivables fell even as revenue rose sharply, consistent with a subsequent correction/write-down of previously overstated receivable balances.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNet=1879700000 (2012-12-31) vs Revenues=9024900000 (FY2012); AccountsReceivableNet=1512600000 (2013-12-31) vs Revenues=10771900000 (FY2013)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K/A balance sheet and income statement FY2012-2013

affected_line_items: Multiple - annual report line items generally
mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Recurring inability to file annual reports on time (three NT 10-K notifications across 2009, 2013, and 2014) combined with an unprecedented one-day-later 10-K/A amendment in March 2014 indicates a pattern of internal control weaknesses or last-minute accounting corrections that increases the risk of misstatement across multiple periods, though the specific misstated accounts beyond goodwill/intangibles/receivables cannot be identified from the data provided.

top_signals: 10-K/A filed only one day after the original FY2013 10-K (2014-03-19 -> 2014-03-20), an extremely unusual amendment turnaround suggesting a rapid correction of a known error
Three separate NT 10-K late-filing notifications (2009-03-03, 2013-03-04, 2014-03-03) indicating recurring reporting/control difficulties

Goodwill and Intangible assets roughly tripled and nearly doubled respectively between the last pre-10-K/A interim filing (Q3 2012) and the FY2012 figures reported in the 2014 10-K/A, far outpacing ~9% revenue growth
Receivables grew materially faster than revenue in 2011-2012 then reversed sharply in 2013 despite continued revenue growth, a pattern consistent with prior overstatement followed by correction
Persistent, large gap between net income/profit and operating cash flow across all years, though partly attributable to capital-intensive fleet depreciation
